title: Monotonic constrained tree specialist on wear and finish-feasibility variables
summary: Official LightGBM/XGBoost docs note monotone constraints can improve predictive performance when strong directional priors exist.
rationale: Official LightGBM/XGBoost docs note monotone constraints can improve predictive performance when strong directional priors exist. This task has several plausible monotone effects after conditioning: more compound-window overshoot, larger positive lap-time loss, and higher degradation should not reduce pit risk.
implementation_hint: Train one specialist GBDT using only high-confidence directional features such as `TyreLife_ToFinish_CompoundRatio`, `Compound_LapsBeyondFinishWindow`, positive `LapTime_Delta`, degradation-per-lap, and maybe inverse margins-to-finish, with monotone signs enforced. Blend its OOF predictions with the unconstrained main model.
expected_effect: Often improves public-board stability by removing implausible local inversions while contributing diversity to the ensemble.
risk: Some raw features are not globally monotone because of fresh-tyre resets and wet-race regime changes; constraints should be applied only to carefully transformed variables and maybe only on dry-race subsets.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
